You communicate clearly, admit uncertainty when appropriate, and prioritize being genuinely useful over being verbose unless otherwise directed below. Be targeted and efficient in your exploration and investigations.

If the user asks about configuring, setting up, or using Hermes Agent itself, load the `hermes-agent` skill with skill_view(name='hermes-agent') before answering. Docs: https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs

Conversation started: Thursday, May 07, 2026 02:05 PM
Model: deepseek-chat
Provider: custom

You are a CLI AI Agent. Try not to use markdown but simple text renderable inside a terminal. File delivery: there is no attachment channel — the user reads your response directly in their terminal. Do NOT emit MEDIA:/path tags (those are only intercepted on messaging platforms like Telegram, Discord, Slack, etc.; on the CLI they render as literal text). When referring to a file you created or changed, just state its absolute path in plain text; the user can open it from there.

function.name: mcp_brave_search_brave_image_search
function.description: Performs an image search using the Brave Search API. Helpful for when you need pictures of people, places, things, graphic design ideas, art inspiration, and more. When relaying results in a markdown environment, it may be helpful to include images in the results (e.g., ![image.title](image.properties.url)).

query.description: The user's search query. Query cannot be empty. Limited to 400 characters and 50 words.

country.description: Search query country, where the results come from. The country string is limited to 2 character country codes of supported countries.

search_lang.description: Search language preference. The 2 or more character language code for which the search results are provided.

count.description: Number of results (1-200, default 50). Combine this parameter with `offset` to paginate search results.

safesearch.description: Filters search results for adult content. The following values are supported: 'off' - No filtering. 'strict' - Drops all adult content from search results.

function.name: mcp_brave_search_brave_local_search
function.description:
Brave Local Search API provides enrichments for location search results. Access to this API is available only through the Brave Search API Pro plans; confirm the user's plan before using this tool (if the user does not have a Pro plan, use the brave_web_search tool). Searches for local businesses and places using Brave's Local Search API. Best for queries related to physical locations, businesses, restaurants, services, etc.
    
    Returns detailed information including:
        - Business names and addresses
        - Ratings and review counts
        - Phone numbers and opening hours

    Use this when the query implies 'near me', 'in my area', or mentions specific locations (e.g., 'in San Francisco'). This tool automatically falls back to brave_web_search if no local results are found.

country.description: Search query country, where the results come from. The country string is limited to 2 character country codes of supported countries.

search_lang.description: Search language preference. The 2 or more character language code for which the search results are provided.

ui_lang.description: The language of the UI. The 2 or more character language code for which the search results are provided.

count.description: Number of results (1-20, default 10). Applies only to web search results (i.e., has no effect on locations, news, videos, etc.)

safesearch.description: Filters search results for adult content. The following values are supported: 'off' - No filtering. 'moderate' - Filters explicit content (e.g., images and videos), but allows adult domains in search results. 'strict' - Drops all adult content from search results. The default value is 'moderate'.

freshness.description: Filters search results by when they were discovered. The following values are supported: 'pd' - Discovered within the last 24 hours. 'pw' - Discovered within the last 7 days. 'pm' - Discovered within the last 31 days. 'py' - Discovered within the last 365 days. 'YYYY-MM-DDtoYYYY-MM-DD' - Timeframe is also supported by specifying the date range e.g. [REDACTED_PHONE]to[REDACTED_PHONE].

text_decorations.description: Whether display strings (e.g. result snippets) should include decoration markers (e.g. highlighting characters).

spellcheck.description: Whether to spellcheck the provided query.

goggles.description: Goggles act as a custom re-ranking on top of Brave's search index. The parameter supports both a url where the Goggle is hosted or the definition of the Goggle. For more details, refer to the Goggles repository (i.e., https://github.com/brave/goggles-quickstart).

units.description: The measurement units. If not provided, units are derived from search country.

extra_snippets.description: A snippet is an excerpt from a page you get as a result of the query, and extra_snippets allow you to get up to 5 additional, alternative excerpts. Only available under Free AI, Base AI, Pro AI, Base Data, Pro Data and Custom plans.

summary.description: This parameter enables summary key generation in web search results. This is required for summarizer to be enabled.

function.name: mcp_brave_search_brave_news_search
function.description:
This tool searches for news articles using Brave's News Search API based on the user's query. Use it when you need current news information, breaking news updates, or articles about specific topics, events, or entities.
    
    When to use:
        - Finding recent news articles on specific topics
        - Getting breaking news updates
        - Researching current events or trending stories
        - Gathering news sources and headlines for analysis

    Returns a JSON list of news-related results with title, url, and description. Some results may contain snippets of text from the article.
    
    When relaying results in markdown-supporting environments, always cite sources with hyperlinks.
    
    Examples:
        - "According to [Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/technology/china-bans/), China bans uncertified and recalled power banks on planes".
        - "The [New York Times](https://www.nytimes.com/2025/06/27/us/technology/ev-sales.html) reports that Tesla's EV sales have increased by 20%".
        - "According to [BBC News](https://www.bbc.com/news/world-europe-65910000), the UK government has announced a new policy to support renewable energy".

country.description: Search query country, where the results come from. The country string is limited to 2 character country codes of supported countries.

search_lang.description: Search language preference. The 2 or more character language code for which the search results are provided.

ui_lang.description: User interface language preferred in response. Usually of the format <language_code>-<country_code>. For more, see RFC 9110.

safesearch.description: Filters search results for adult content. The following values are supported: 'off' - No filtering. 'moderate' - Filter out explicit content. 'strict' - Filter out explicit and suggestive content. The default value is 'moderate'.

freshness.description: Filters search results by when they were discovered. The following values are supported: 'pd' - Discovered within the last 24 hours. 'pw' - Discovered within the last 7 Days. 'pm' - Discovered within the last 31 Days. 'py' - Discovered within the last 365 Days. 'YYYY-MM-DDtoYYYY-MM-DD' - Timeframe is also supported by specifying the date range e.g. [REDACTED_PHONE]to[REDACTED_PHONE].

extra_snippets.description: A snippet is an excerpt from a page you get as a result of the query, and extra_snippets allow you to get up to 5 additional, alternative excerpts. Only available under Free AI, Base AI, Pro AI, Base Data, Pro Data and Custom plans.

goggles.description: Goggles act as a custom re-ranking on top of Brave's search index. The parameter supports both a url where the Goggle is hosted or the definition of the Goggle. For more details, refer to the Goggles repository (i.e., https://github.com/brave/goggles-quickstart).

function.name: mcp_brave_search_brave_summarizer
function.description:
Retrieves AI-generated summaries of web search results using Brave's Summarizer API. This tool processes search results to create concise, coherent summaries of information gathered from multiple sources.

    When to use:

    - When you need a concise overview of complex topics from multiple sources
    - For quick fact-checking or getting key points without reading full articles
    - When providing users with summarized information that synthesizes various perspectives
    - For research tasks requiring distilled information from web searches

    Returns a text summary that consolidates information from the search results. Optional features include inline references to source URLs and additional entity information.

    Requirements: Must first perform a web search using brave_web_search with summary=true parameter. Requires a Pro AI subscription to access the summarizer functionality.

key.description: The key is equal to value of field key as part of the Summarizer response model.

entity_info.description: Returns extra entities info with the summary response.

inline_references.description: Adds inline references to the summary response.

function.name: mcp_brave_search_brave_video_search
function.description:
Searches for videos using Brave's Video Search API and returns structured video results with metadata.

    When to use:
        - When you need to find videos related to a specific topic, keyword, or query.
        - Useful for discovering video content, getting video metadata, or finding videos from specific creators/publishers.

    Returns a JSON list of video-related results with title, url, description, duration, and thumbnail_url.

function.name: mcp_brave_search_brave_web_search
function.description:
Performs web searches using the Brave Search API and returns comprehensive search results with rich metadata.

    When to use:
        - General web searches for information, facts, or current topics
        - Location-based queries (restaurants, businesses, points of interest)
        - News searches for recent events or breaking stories
        - Finding videos, discussions, or FAQ content
        - Research requiring diverse result types (web pages, images, reviews, etc.)

    Returns a JSON list of web results with title, description, and URL.
    
    When the "results_filter" parameter is empty, JSON results may also contain FAQ, Discussions, News, and Video results.

function.name: mcp_context7_query_docs
function.description:
Retrieves and queries up-to-date documentation and code examples from Context7 for any programming library or framework.

You must call 'Resolve Context7 Library ID' tool first to obtain the exact Context7-compatible library ID required to use this tool, UNLESS the user explicitly provides a library ID in the format '/org/project' or '/org/project/version' in their query.

Do not call this tool more than 3 times per question.

libraryId.description: Exact Context7-compatible library ID (e.g., '/mongodb/docs', '/vercel/next.js', '/supabase/supabase', '/vercel/next.js/v14.3.0-canary.87') retrieved from 'resolve-library-id' or directly from user query in the format '/org/project' or '/org/project/version'.

query.description: The question or task you need help with. Be specific and include relevant details. Good: 'How to set up authentication with JWT in Express.js' or 'React useEffect cleanup function examples'. Bad: 'auth' or 'hooks'. The query is sent to the Context7 API for processing. Do not include any sensitive or confidential information such as API keys, passwords, credentials, personal data, or proprietary code in your query.

function.name: mcp_context7_read_resource
function.description: Read a resource by URI from MCP server 'context7'

uri.description: URI of the resource to read

function.name: mcp_context7_resolve_library_id
function.description:
Resolves a package/product name to a Context7-compatible library ID and returns matching libraries.

You MUST call this function before 'Query Documentation' tool to obtain a valid Context7-compatible library ID UNLESS the user explicitly provides a library ID in the format '/org/project' or '/org/project/version' in their query.

Each result includes:
- Library ID: Context7-compatible identifier (format: /org/project)
- Name: Library or package name
- Description: Short summary
- Code Snippets: Number of available code examples
- Source Reputation: Authority indicator (High, Medium, Low, or Unknown)
- Benchmark Score: Quality indicator (100 is the highest score)
- Versions: List of versions if available. Use one of those versions if the user provides a version in their query. The format of the version is /org/project/version.

For best results, select libraries based on name match, source reputation, snippet coverage, benchmark score, and relevance to your use case.

Selection Process:
1. Analyze the query to understand what library/package the user is looking for
2. Return the most relevant match based on:
- Name similarity to the query (exact matches prioritized)
- Description relevance to the query's intent
- Documentation coverage (prioritize libraries with higher Code Snippet counts)
- Source reputation (consider libraries with High or Medium reputation more authoritative)
- Benchmark Score: Quality indicator (100 is the highest score)

Response Format:
- Return the selected library ID in a clearly marked section
- Provide a brief explanation for why this library was chosen
- If multiple good matches exist, acknowledge this but proceed with the most relevant one
- If no good matches exist, clearly state this and suggest query refinements

For ambiguous queries, request clarification before proceeding with a best-guess match.

IMPORTANT: Do not call this tool more than 3 times per question. If you cannot find what you need after 3 calls, use the best result you have.

query.description: The question or task you need help with. This is used to rank library results by relevance to what the user is trying to accomplish. The query is sent to the Context7 API for processing. Do not include any sensitive or confidential information such as API keys, passwords, credentials, personal data, or proprietary code in your query.

libraryName.description: Library name to search for and retrieve a Context7-compatible library ID. Use the official library name with proper punctuation — e.g., 'Next.js' instead of 'nextjs', 'Customer.io' instead of 'customerio', 'Three.js' instead of 'threejs'.

function.name: mcp_exa_web_fetch_exa
function.description:
Read a webpage's full content as clean markdown. Use after web_search_exa when highlights are insufficient or to read any URL.

Best for: Extracting full content from known URLs. Batch multiple URLs in one call.
Returns: Clean text content and metadata from the page(s).

urls.description: URLs to read. Batch multiple URLs in one call.

maxCharacters.description: Maximum characters to extract per page (must be a positive number, default: 3000)

function.name: mcp_exa_web_search_exa
function.description:
Search the web for any topic and get clean, ready-to-use content.

      Best for: Finding current information, news, facts, people, companies, or answering questions about any topic.
      Returns: Clean text content from top search results.

      Query tips: 
      describe the ideal page, not keywords. "blog post comparing React and Vue performance" not "React vs Vue".
      Use category:people / category:company to search through Linkedin profiles / companies respectively.
      If highlights are insufficient, follow up with web_fetch_exa on the best URLs.

query.description: Natural language search query. Should be a semantically rich description of the ideal page, not just keywords. Optionally include category:<type> (company, people) to focus results — e.g. 'category:people John Doe software engineer'.

numResults.description: Number of search results to return (must be a number, default: 10).

function.name: mcp_filesystem_create_directory
function.description: Create a new directory or ensure a directory exists. Can create multiple nested directories in one operation. If the directory already exists, this operation will succeed silently. Perfect for setting up directory structures for projects or ensuring required paths exist. Only works within allowed directories.

function.name: mcp_filesystem_directory_tree
function.description: Get a recursive tree view of files and directories as a JSON structure. Each entry includes 'name', 'type' (file/directory), and 'children' for directories. Files have no children array, while directories always have a children array (which may be empty). The output is formatted with 2-space indentation for readability. Only works within allowed directories.

function.name: [REDACTED_TOKEN]
function.description:
A detailed tool for dynamic and reflective problem-solving through thoughts.
This tool helps analyze problems through a flexible thinking process that can adapt and evolve.
Each thought can build on, question, or revise previous insights as understanding deepens.

When to use this tool:
- Breaking down complex problems into steps
- Planning and design with room for revision
- Analysis that might need course correction
- Problems where the full scope might not be clear initially
- Problems that require a multi-step solution
- Tasks that need to maintain context over multiple steps
- Situations where irrelevant information needs to be filtered out

Key features:
- You can adjust total_thoughts up or down as you progress
- You can question or revise previous thoughts
- You can add more thoughts even after reaching what seemed like the end
- You can express uncertainty and explore alternative approaches
- Not every thought needs to build linearly - you can branch or backtrack
- Generates a solution hypothesis
- Verifies the hypothesis based on the Chain of Thought steps
- Repeats the process until satisfied
- Provides a correct answer

Parameters explained:
- thought: Your current thinking step, which can include:
  * Regular analytical steps
  * Revisions of previous thoughts
  * Questions about previous decisions
  * Realizations about needing more analysis
  * Changes in approach
  * Hypothesis generation
  * Hypothesis verification
- nextThoughtNeeded: True if you need more thinking, even if at what seemed like the end
- thoughtNumber: Current number in sequence (can go beyond initial total if needed)
- totalThoughts: Current estimate of thoughts needed (can be adjusted up/down)
- isRevision: A boolean indicating if this thought revises previous thinking
- revisesThought: If is_revision is true, which thought number is being reconsidered
- branchFromThought: If branching, which thought number is the branching point
- branchId: Identifier for the current branch (if any)
- needsMoreThoughts: If reaching end but realizing more thoughts needed

You should:
1. Start with an initial estimate of needed thoughts, but be ready to adjust
2. Feel free to question or revise previous thoughts
3. Don't hesitate to add more thoughts if needed, even at the "end"
4. Express uncertainty when present
5. Mark thoughts that revise previous thinking or branch into new paths
6. Ignore information that is irrelevant to the current step
7. Generate a solution hypothesis when appropriate
8. Verify the hypothesis based on the Chain of Thought steps
9. Repeat the process until satisfied with the solution
10. Provide a single, ideally correct answer as the final output
11. Only set nextThoughtNeeded to false when truly done and a satisfactory answer is reached

nextThoughtNeeded.description: Whether another thought step is needed

totalThoughts.description: Estimated total thoughts needed (numeric value, e.g., 5, 10)

function.name: patch
function.description:
Targeted find-and-replace edits in files. Use this instead of sed/awk in terminal. Uses fuzzy matching (9 strategies) so minor whitespace/indentation differences won't break it. Returns a unified diff. Auto-runs syntax checks after editing.

Replace mode (default): find a unique string and replace it.
Patch mode: apply V4A multi-file patches for bulk changes.

mode.description: Edit mode: 'replace' for targeted find-and-replace, 'patch' for V4A multi-file patches

path.description: File path to edit (required for 'replace' mode)

old_string.description: Text to find in the file (required for 'replace' mode). Must be unique in the file unless replace_all=true. Include enough surrounding context to ensure uniqueness.

new_string.description: Replacement text (required for 'replace' mode). Can be empty string to delete the matched text.

replace_all.description: Replace all occurrences instead of requiring a unique match (default: false)

patch.description:
V4A format patch content (required for 'patch' mode). Format:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: path/to/file
@@ context hint @@
 context line
-removed line
+added line
*** End Patch

function.name: process
function.description: Manage background processes started with terminal(background=true). Actions: 'list' (show all), 'poll' (check status + new output), 'log' (full output with pagination), 'wait' (block until done or timeout), 'kill' (terminate), 'write' (send raw stdin data without newline), 'submit' (send data + Enter, for answering prompts), 'close' (close stdin/send EOF).

action.description: Action to perform on background processes

session_id.description: Process session ID (from terminal background output). Required for all actions except 'list'.

data.description: Text to send to process stdin (for 'write' and 'submit' actions)

timeout.description: Max seconds to block for 'wait' action. Returns partial output on timeout.

offset.description: Line offset for 'log' action (default: last 200 lines)

limit.description: Max lines to return for 'log' action

function.name: read_file
function.description: Read a text file with line numbers and pagination. Use this instead of cat/head/tail in terminal. Output format: 'LINE_NUM|CONTENT'. Suggests similar filenames if not found. Use offset and limit for large files. Reads exceeding ~100K characters are rejected; use offset and limit to read specific sections of large files. NOTE: Cannot read images or binary files — use vision_analyze for images.

path.description: Path to the file to read (absolute, relative, or ~/path)

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed, default: 1)

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read (default: 500, max: 2000)

function.name: search_files
function.description:
Search file contents or find files by name. Use this instead of grep/rg/find/ls in terminal. Ripgrep-backed, faster than shell equivalents.

Content search (target='content'): Regex search inside files. Output modes: full matches with line numbers, file paths only, or match counts.

File search (target='files'): Find files by glob pattern (e.g., '*.py', '*config*'). Also use this instead of ls — results sorted by modification time.

pattern.description: Regex pattern for content search, or glob pattern (e.g., '*.py') for file search

target.description: 'content' searches inside file contents, 'files' searches for files by name

path.description: Directory or file to search in (default: current working directory)

file_glob.description: Filter files by pattern in grep mode (e.g., '*.py' to only search Python files)

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 50)

offset.description: Skip first N results for pagination (default: 0)

output_mode.description: Output format for grep mode: 'content' shows matching lines with line numbers, 'files_only' lists file paths, 'count' shows match counts per file

context.description: Number of context lines before and after each match (grep mode only)

function.name: terminal
function.description:
Execute shell commands on a Linux environment. Filesystem usually persists between calls.

Do NOT use cat/head/tail to read files — use read_file instead.
Do NOT use grep/rg/find to search — use search_files instead.
Do NOT use ls to list directories — use search_files(target='files') instead.
Do NOT use sed/awk to edit files — use patch instead.
Do NOT use echo/cat heredoc to create files — use write_file instead.
Reserve terminal for: builds, installs, git, processes, scripts, network, package managers, and anything that needs a shell.

Foreground (default): Commands return INSTANTLY when done, even if the timeout is high. Set timeout=300 for long builds/scripts — you'll still get the result in seconds if it's fast. Prefer foreground for short commands.
Background: Set background=true to get a session_id. Two patterns:
  (1) Long-lived processes that never exit (servers, watchers).
  (2) Long-running tasks with notify_on_complete=true — you can keep working on other things and the system auto-notifies you when the task finishes. Great for test suites, builds, deployments, or anything that takes more than a minute.
For servers/watchers, do NOT use shell-level background wrappers (nohup/disown/setsid/trailing '&') in foreground mode. Use background=true so Hermes can track lifecycle and output.
After starting a server, verify readiness with a health check or log signal, then run tests in a separate terminal() call. Avoid blind sleep loops.
Use process(action="poll") for progress checks, process(action="wait") to block until done.
Working directory: Use 'workdir' for per-command cwd.
PTY mode: Set pty=true for interactive CLI tools (Codex, Claude Code, Python REPL).

Do NOT use vim/nano/interactive tools without pty=true — they hang without a pseudo-terminal. Pipe git output to cat if it might page.

command.description: The command to execute on the VM

background.description: Run the command in the background. Two patterns: (1) Long-lived processes that never exit (servers, watchers). (2) Long-running tasks paired with notify_on_complete=true — you can keep working and get notified when the task finishes. For short commands, prefer foreground with a generous timeout instead.

timeout.description: Max seconds to wait (default: 180, foreground max: 600). Returns INSTANTLY when command finishes — set high for long tasks, you won't wait unnecessarily. Foreground timeout above 600s is rejected; use background=true for longer commands.

workdir.description: Working directory for this command (absolute path). Defaults to the session working directory.

pty.description: Run in pseudo-terminal (PTY) mode for interactive CLI tools like Codex, Claude Code, or Python REPL. Only works with local and SSH backends. Default: false.

notify_on_complete.description: When true (and background=true), you'll be automatically notified exactly once when the process finishes. **This is the right choice for almost every long-running task** — tests, builds, deployments, multi-item batch jobs, anything that takes over a minute and has a defined end. Use this and keep working on other things; the system notifies you on exit. MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE with watch_patterns — when both are set, watch_patterns is dropped.

watch_patterns.description: Strings to watch for in background process output. HARD RATE LIMIT: at most 1 notification per 15 seconds per process — matches arriving inside the cooldown are dropped. After 3 consecutive 15-second windows with dropped matches, watch_patterns is automatically disabled for that process and promoted to notify_on_complete behavior (one notification on exit, no more mid-process spam). USE ONLY for truly rare, one-shot mid-process signals on LONG-LIVED processes that will never exit on their own — e.g. ['Application startup complete'] on a server so you know when to hit its endpoint, or ['migration done'] on a daemon. DO NOT use for: (1) end-of-run markers like 'DONE'/'PASS' — use notify_on_complete instead; (2) error patterns like 'ERROR'/'Traceback' in loops or multi-item batch jobs — they fire on every iteration and you'll hit the strike limit fast; (3) anything you'd ever combine with notify_on_complete. When in doubt, choose notify_on_complete. MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE with notify_on_complete — set one, not both.

function.name: write_file
function.description: Write content to a file, completely replacing existing content. Use this instead of echo/cat heredoc in terminal. Creates parent directories automatically. OVERWRITES the entire file — use 'patch' for targeted edits. Auto-runs syntax checks on .py/.json/.yaml/.toml and other linted languages; only NEW errors introduced by this write are surfaced (pre-existing errors are filtered out).

path.description: Path to the file to write (will be created if it doesn't exist, overwritten if it does)

content.description: Complete content to write to the file

messages.content:
Create documentation files in /root/nexa-paraguay/docs/. Read the existing files first, then create concise, well-structured versions in the new locations.

Files to create (copy from existing, clean up, structure properly):

1. docs/01-client/stakeholder-qa.md
   Read /root/nexa-paraguay/docs/STAKEHOLDER-QA.md first. Create a cleaned-up version.
   
2. docs/01-client/stakeholder-review.md
   Read /root/nexa-paraguay/docs/STAKEHOLDER-REVIEW.md. Cleaned-up version.
   
3. docs/02-site/deep-audit.md
   Read /root/nexa-paraguay/docs/DEEP_AUDIT.md. Keep the technical content.
   
4. docs/02-site/improvement-plan.md
   Read /root/nexa-paraguay/docs/IMPROVEMENT_PLAN.md. Structured version.
   
5. docs/02-site/dns.md
   Read /root/nexa-paraguay/docs/DNS.md. DNS config.
   
6. docs/03-brand/brand-guide.md
   Read /root/nexa-paraguay/docs/brand-guide.md and site.json + tokens.json from repo root. Expand with real detail.
   
7. docs/03-brand/social-assets.md
   Read /root/nexa-paraguay/docs/SOCIAL_ASSETS.md. Keep structure intact.
   
8. docs/04-images/images-manifest.md
   Read /root/nexa-paraguay/images.json. Document all 111 images by category with descriptions.
   
9. docs/05-content/content-locales.md
   Read /root/nexa-paraguay/content/en.json (just head). Document the 4-locale structure.
   
10. docs/05-content/blog-posts.md
    List all blog posts from /root/nexa-paraguay/blog/ directory. Catalog each.

For each file, add a header with purpose, last updated date, and cross-refs to related docs. Keep files focused on ONE topic each.
I'll start by reading all the source files and exploring the repository structure.

messages.name: read_file
messages.content:
{"content": "     1|# Nexa Paraguay — stakeholder Q&A packet
     2|
     3|**For:** Nexa Paraguay leadership (commercial director Europe, operations director Paraguay, legal counsel).
     4|**Audience:** decision-makers who need to confirm, modify, or reject the defaults we've built into the site.
     5|**Purpose:** every open question that blocks production launch, with our proposed answer grounded in the codebase so you can confirm "yes, ship it" or redline what to change.
     6|**How to use:** read each section, confirm the answer in the "**Proposed answer**" block, or respond with changes. Anything marked 🔴 is a blocker; 🟡 is ship-ready but improvable; 🟢 is done and just needs sign-off.
     7|
     8|Cross-references:
     9|- `LAUNCH.md` — the 12 blocking items we tracked during build.
    10|- `STAKEHOLDER-REVIEW.md` — per-role checklist version of this doc.
    11|- `DNS.md` — DNS cutover sequence.
    12|
    13|Staging URL: `https://staging.nexaparaguay.com`
    14|Proposed production URL: `https://nexaparaguay.com`
    15|
    16|---
    17|
    18|## Legend
    19|
    20|| Symbol | Meaning |
    21||--------|---------|
    22|| 🔴 | Blocks production launch — we need an answer before cutover |
    23|| 🟡 | Built with a reasonable default, improvable later |
    24|| 🟢 | Fully built, just needs your "go" |
    25|| 📌 | A choice you should make consciously; we can't pick for you |
    26|
    27|---
    28|
    29|# Section A — Brand & identity
    30|
    31|## A1. Company name 🔴
    32|
    33|**Question:** What's the final legal and commercial name to use across the site, footer, copyright, email signatures, and legal documents?
    34|
    35|**Proposed answer:** "**Nexa Paraguay**" — single two-word brand, used in:
    36|- `siteName` in all 4 locale content files (`es.json`, `en.json`, `nl.json`, `de.json`)
    37|- `navigation.businessName` on every page header
    38|- Footer: `© 2026 Nexa Paraguay. Todos los derechos reservados.`
    39|- WhatsApp message prefill: "Hola, soy un lead de Nexa Paraguay…"
    40|- Email `From:` name: "Nexa Paraguay"
    41|- Social handles: `linkedin.com/company/nexa-paraguay`, `instagram.com/nexaparaguay`
    42|- Legal entity in privacy policy: currently "Nexa Paraguay" as placeholder
    43|
    44|**Confirm:**
    45|- [ ] The commercial name is exactly "Nexa Paraguay" (not "NEXA Paraguay" all-caps, not "Nexa LATAM", not "Nexa Group Paraguay", etc.)?
    46|- [ ] The legal entity (the company on invoices, contracts, and privacy policy) has the **same** name, or a different one we need to name separately?
    47|- [ ] Is there a tagline to add alongside? Current NL tagline: "Uw nieuwe start in Paraguay, eenvoudig en gerust." ("Your new start in Paraguay, simple and calm.") Confirm or replace.
    48|
    49|**Consequences of change:** rename is a one-line edit per locale; deferrable until cutover.
    50|
    51|---
    52|
    53|## A2. Logo 🔴
    54|
    55|**Question:** What logo file should ship? Do you have an SVG / PNG suite?
    56|
    57|**Proposed answer (current state):** Text-only wordmark — "Nexa Paraguay" rendered in Playfair Display, navy `#1B2A4A`. No icon, no SVG. Used in the header and footer. This is a temporary placeholder.
    58|
    59|**What we need:**
    60|- [ ] Logo SVG (preferred) or high-res PNG (2048px wide) with transparent background
    61|- [ ] Icon-only mark for favicon + social share thumbnails (32×32 min, 512×512 recommended)
    62|- [ ] Monochrome (white) version for dark backgrounds (hero, CTA banner)
    63|- [ ] Favicon file (`favicon.ico`) + Apple touch icon (180×180 PNG)
    64|
    65|**If you haven't commissioned one yet:** we can keep the wordmark through soft-launch. The site still looks intentional — the placeholder isn't broken, just generic.
    66|
    67|**Delivery path:** drop files into `sites/nexa-paraguay/images/brand/` with names `logo.svg`, `logo-dark.svg`, `logo-icon.svg`, `favicon.ico`, `apple-touch-icon.png`. We wire them in.
    68|
    69|---
    70|
    71|## A3. Color palette 🟢
    72|
    73|**Question:** Do the current colors match the brand direction, or do you want to change the primary/secondary?
    74|
    75|**Proposed answer (current state):**
    76|
    77|| Token | Hex | Usage |
    78||-------|-----|-------|
    79|| `--primary` | `#1B2A4A` | Deep navy — hero background, CTA banner, headings |
    80|| `--secondary` | `#C9A96E` | Warm gold — buttons, accents, "Más elegido" badge |
    81|| `--accent` | `#C9A96E` | (same as secondary) |
    82|| `--background` | `#FFFFFF` | Page background |
    83|| `--surface` | `#FFFFFF` | Card background |
    84|| `--surface-light` | `#F5F3EE` | Alt section background (warm off-white) |
    85|| `--text` | `#1B2A4A` | Body text |
    86|| `--text-muted` | `#777777` | Captions, secondary copy |
    87|| `--color-success` | `#16a34a` | Form success states |
    88|| `--color-error` | `#dc2626` | Form errors |
    89|
    90|Palette is in `src/verticals/relocacion/defaults.tokens.json`. Vibe: **institutional, trust-coded, European-feel** (navy + gold).
    91|
    92|**Confirm:**

     2|
     3|Staging URL: `https://staging.nexaparaguay.com`
     4|
     5|## Checklist for commercial director (Europe)
     6|
     7|- [ ] NL hero copy: "Vestig uw onderneming in Paraguay." — correct?
     8|- [ ] NL subhead mentions all four deliverables (residence/company/bank/land) — correct?
     9|- [ ] Programs comparison: 4 tiers ordered Base → Business → Investor → Tierras; Business marked "Meest gekozen" — OK?
    10|- [ ] Pricing: shows "USD 4.400+" and "USD 6.900+" with note about final Nexa price TBD — OK?
    11|- [ ] Process: 5 steps, total duration "8–12 weken" — accurate?
    12|- [ ] FAQ: 15 items present — review wording for NL and EN (DE flagged for translator polish).
    13|- [ ] Contact form: 7 fields, privacy checkbox required — OK?
    14|- [ ] Cookie banner: EN/NL/DE/ES localised — text acceptable?
    15|- [ ] Blog: 10 Spanish seeds — which 3 to prioritise for translation first?
    16|
    17|## Checklist for operations director (Paraguay)
    18|
    19|- [ ] Asunción photography plan — commissioned shoot dates?
    20|- [ ] WhatsApp number `+595 XXX XXX XXX` — which number goes live?
    21|- [ ] Calendly event "Consulta gratuita 30 min" created on the team calendar?
    22|- [ ] Team member names + roles on `/sobre` — replace roles-only placeholders with real names?
    23|
    24|## Checklist for legal

    28|- [ ] `/programas` Compra-de-Tierras section — legal risk of "Conversación exploratoria" CTA absent scope?
    29|- [ ] SEPRELAD (AML) compliance status for lead handling?
    30|

    35|- [ ] Mailchimp audience tag "nexa-paraguay-lead" + Customer Journey for the 7-email nurture — sequence activated?
    36|- [ ] LinkedIn company page + Instagram @nexaparaguay — reserved?
    37|
    38|## UX spot-checks on staging (per locale)
    39|
    40|For each of `nl` / `en` / `de` / `es`:
    41|
    42|- [ ] Home → Contact CTA works, scrolls/redirects correctly.
    43|- [ ] Language switcher in header preserves current page across locales.
    44|- [ ] `/programas` comparison matrix renders all 4 tiers on desktop and stacks on mobile.
    45|- [ ] `/proceso` timeline renders 5 steps without overflow.
    46|- [ ] Footer has correct copyright year + privacy link.
    47|- [ ] Cookie banner only appears on first visit; choice persists on refresh.

{"content": " 1|# Nexa Paraguay — Deep Code Quality & Architecture Analysis 2| 3|## 🔴 P0: BREAKING / DATA LOSS RISK 4| 5|### 1. Blog image keys don't match (content → images.json mismatch) 6|**Issue**: Blog posts reference `image` field like `"residencia-2025"` but images.json blog keys are `residencia2024`, `propiedades`, `banca`, `emprender`, `costOfLiving` — completely different naming. BlogSection uses `resolveImage(images, '@img:blog.${post.image}')` which looks for `images.blog['residencia-2025']` — doesn't exist. All 6 blog posts show no cover images. 7|**Fix**: Update `es.json` blog post `image` fields to match images.json keys: `residencia2024`, `propiedades`, `banca`, `emprender`, `costOfLiving`, `healthcare`, `schools`, `neighborhoods`. 8| 9|### 2. buildPageContent still runs but most components skip it 10|**Issue**: Both `index.tsx` and `[slug].tsx` run `buildPageContent(content)` on every render, iterating all sections and doing deep key resolution. But the dispatch now passes `sectionData` directly to each component. `buildPageContent` creates a redundant `pageContent` object that's only used by sections that don't have dedicated components (fallback path). It runs 3+ `reduce()` calls per section — wasted CPU on every request. 11|**Fix**: Remove `buildPageContent` entirely. The dispatch already does `resolveContent(content, section.content || section.id)` which gets the exact data needed. 12| 13|### 3. readFileSync reads 3+ JSON files per request — no caching 14|**Issue**: `getServerSideProps` reads `content/es.json` (~100KB), `images.json` (~47KB), plus page config JSON on EVERY request. No memoization, no cache. For 24+ routes at 350ms avg response, this adds significant disk I/O. 15|**Fix**: Add simple in-memory cache map or use Next.js `res.setHeader('Cache-Control')`. 16| 17|### 4. `/programas` duplicates homepage services section 18|**Issue**: `programas.json` includes `{"id":"services","content":"home.services"}` — the full 9-item services section from the homepage is duplicated on the programs page. This means scrolling down on /programas shows the same content as the homepage. 19|**Fix**: Remove that section from `programas.json`. 20| 21|### 5. `compliance-disclaimer-footer` section has no component 22|**Issue**: `contacto.json` has `{"id":"compliance-disclaimer-footer","content":"complianceDisclaimer"}` — no component in SECTION_MAP. Falls through to generic fallback. `resolveContent(content, 'complianceDisclaimer')` looks for `content['complianceDisclaimer']` which doesn't exist in es.json (it might be named differently). 23|**Fix**: Either add a component or remove from page config. 24| 25|--- 26| 27|## 🟠 P1: HIGH — affects reliability, maintainability, UX 28| 29|### 6. 0% TypeScript coverage — 48 `: any` types 30|**Issue**: Every component interface uses `: any` for props. No type checking on content structure, section data, images manifest. A content key typo (`'memmberImage'` instead of `'memberImage'`) won't be caught until runtime. 31|**Fix**: Define proper TypeScript types for: 32|- Content structure (Content, PageConfig, Footer, Navigation, Stats, Testimonial, etc.) 33|- Image manifest (ImagesManifest, ImageEntry) 34|- Section component props (SectionProps) 35|- Component data shapes (FaqItem, BlogPost, TeamMember, Pillar, ProgramTier, etc.) 36| 37|Estimated types needed: ~30 interfaces/types across 5 files. 38| 39|### 7. Pages router duplication — index.tsx + [slug].tsx 90% identical 40|**Issue**: Both files share SECTION_MAP, SLUG_MAP, loadJSON, similar rendering logic. If you add a section component or change data flow, you must edit both files. Currently out of sync on some items (newsletter-signup, story, team in index.tsx but not [slug].tsx's extras — wait, they ARE in both now? Let me check — yes, but only 3 of 16 extra components are in index.tsx). 41|**Fix**: Merge both pages into one by handling the root `/` case in `[slug].tsx` when `slug === 'home'` or `params?.slug === undefined`. Delete `index.tsx`. 42| 43|### 8. middleware.ts does almost nothing useful 44|**Issue**: 25 lines of boilerplate for a favicon redirect and console logging. No locale routing, no security headers, no redirects. 45|**Fix**: Add CSP headers, locale detection + redirect, and remove favicon redirect (handle in next.config.js). 46| 47|### 9. `GallerySection` uses `resolveImage?.()` with optional chaining — indicates bad typing 48|**Issue**: Line 392: `resolveImage?.(images, ...)` — the `?.` is only needed if TypeScript thinks `resolveImage` might be undefined. It's imported at the top of the file. This is either a linting suppression or stale code from before the import was added. 49|**Fix**: Remove `?.` → `resolveImage(images, ...)`. 50| 51|### 10. `ServicesSection` has `(d as any).groups` cast — type safety broken 52|**Issue**: Line 131: `const groups = d.groups || (d as any).groups || []` — the `(d as any)` indicates the type definition for d doesn't include `groups`. The root cause: `data || pageContent || {}` means `d` could be either the section data (which has `groups`) or the whole `pageContent` object (which doesn't). Since the dispatch now passes `data` correctly, `pageContent` fallback is never used. 53|**Fix**: Remove the `(d as any)` cast and the `pageContent` fallback parameter. ServicesSection only needs `data`. 54| 55|### 11. Blog individual posts have `post.body` placeholder text 56|**Issue**: `es.json` blog posts have `body` as an empty string for all 6 posts. The `blog/[slug].tsx` renders `"Contenido completo próximamente."` because `post.body` is falsy. Blog posts exist as descriptions but have no full article content. 57|**Fix**: Write full article content for each blog post (or generate from the excerpt as a starting point). 58| 59|### 12. Footer logo path hardcoded 60|**Issue**: `Footer.tsx` has `src="/images/brand/logo-dark.svg"` hardcoded instead of resolving through images manifest like other images. 61|**Fix**: Read logo path from content or images.json. 62| 63|### 13. No error boundaries 64|**Issue**: If any section component throws (missing data, malformed content), the entire page renders as 500 white screen. A single bad FAQ item crashes the whole /faq page. 65|**Fix**: Wrap each section in `` that shows a subtle fallback. 66| 67|### 14. `page-content` section id in page configs vs SECTION_MAP 68|**Issue**: Some page configs use section ids that don't exist in SECTION_MAP (e.g. `intake-wizard`, `pills`, `booking-embed` — fixed now). But `pills` on servicios.json has no component. Fallback renders it with basic styling. 69|**Fix**: Either rename to `services` (which has a component), or add `PillsSection` to SECTION_MAP. 70| 71|--- 72| 73|## 🟡 P2: MEDIUM — quality of life improvements 74| 75|### 15. Theme variable naming — `c`, `r`, `s`, `sz` are too terse 76|**Issue**: Destructuring at top of `sections-extra.tsx`: 77|```ts 78|const c = theme.colors, r = theme.radii, s = theme.spacing, sz = theme.sizes 79|``` 80|This makes code hard to read: `c.primary` vs `theme.colors.primary`. New developers won't know what `c`, `r`, `s`, `sz` mean. 81|**Fix**: Use full names: `const { colors, radii, spacing, sizes } = theme`. 82| 83|### 16. HeroSection declares mobileBg but never uses it 84|**Issue**: `HeroSection` calls `resolveImage(images, c.backgroundImageMobile)` and stores it as `mobileBg` but never references it in the JSX. Half the code is dead. 85|**Fix**: Either remove `mobileBg` or add a responsive `` or `@media` background swap for mobile. 86| 87|### 17. `scale(1.02)` on highlighted program tier — layout shift + browser compat 88|**Issue**: `ProgramsSection` applies `transform: tier.highlighted ? 'scale(1.02)' : 'none'`. This causes: 89|- Layout shift when the highlighted card is taller than others 90|- Visual clipping on some browsers (Safari) 91|- Accessibility issue (hover effects on static content) 92|**Fix**: Use `borderWidth: tier.highlighted ? '2px' : '1px'` + accent border color instead of scaling. 93| 94|### 18. Tax calculator placeholder is hardcoded in JSX 95|**Issue**: `TaxCalculatorSection` renders `

Calculadora próximamente.

` as a string literal. Should read from content so it can be updated without code deploy. 96|**Fix**: Use `c.placeholder || 'Calculadora próximamente.'` where `placeholder` is a field in the taxCalculator content object. 97| 98|### 19. `@ai-whisperers/*` local file dependencies — breaks Docker builds 99|**Issue**: 9 packages use `file:/root/ai-whisperers-base/packages/...` paths. These paths don't exist inside Docker context. Docker build only works because `node_modules` is already installed on the VPS and gets included via Docker cache — but a clean build fails. 100|**Fix**: Either (a) publish these packages to npm/GitHub Packages and reference versions, (b) mount `ai-whisperers-base` as Docker build context, or (c) remove unused packages. 101| 102|### 20. `style jsx` in Header.tsx adds runtime overhead 103|**Issue**: The mobile nav uses `` which injects CSS at runtime per component instance. For a sticky header rendered on every page, this is wasted. 104|**Fix**: Move CSS to a static file or use inline styles with a `@media` query approach (the display toggle is already inline — just remove `display: none` CSS and control it purely via React state). 105| 106|### 21. page config mapping uses dots — fragile key resolution 107|**Issue**: Section content keys like `landingEmpresa.programs` are resolved with `key.split('.').reduce(...)`. If a key like `contactPage.booking` has a nested key like `features` that's an array, it still resolves correctly, but if any intermediate key is missing, it silently returns null and the section doesn't render. No warning or logging. 108|**Fix**: Add debug logging when content key resolution fails. 109| 110|### 22. Duplicate services on multiple pages 111|**Issue**: The full services section appears on: homepage (`home.services`), /servicios page (`servicesPage.detail` — correct), /programas page (`home.services` — duplicate). Content is the same. Users see the same 9 service cards 2-3 times. 112|**Fix**: Remove from /programas page config. Keep only on /servicios and homepage. 113| 114|### 23. `loadJSON` defined in 3 files — should be shared utility 115|**Issue**: `loadJSON` function defined identically in `[slug].tsx`, `index.tsx`, and `blog/[slug].tsx`. 12 lines of duplication. 116|**Fix**: Move to `content.ts` or a `utils.ts` file. 117| 118|### 24. Blog `/blog/[slug]` has no `post.body` fallback if body is array 119|**Issue**: The blog post component expects `post.body` to be a string for `split('\n')`. If some blog posts have body as an array of paragraphs (mixed content format), it would crash. 120|**Fix**: Handle both string and array body formats. 121| 122|### 25. No sitemap.xml or robots.txt 123|**Issue**: Site has no sitemap for search engines. No robots.txt either. Google can't discover the 24+ pages. 124|**Fix**: Add a `/sitemap.xml` page that generates an XML sitemap from the page configs and blog posts. 125| 126|### 26. 404 page is Next.js default 127|**Issue**: The default 404 has no branding, no navigation, no helpful suggestions. User lands on `/404` and sees raw text. 128|**Fix**: Create a custom 404 page with logo, search, popular pages. 129| 130|### 27. No GA4 events configured 131|**Issue**: GA4 measurement ID is set (`G-XE49GLEP34`) but no events fire. No `page_view`, no `click`, no `form_submit`. Analytics has zero data. 132|**Fix**: Add `gtag` script to `_document.tsx` or as a component, fire `page_view` on route change, add click handlers for CTAs. 133| 134|### 28. `tsconfig.json` has `strict: true` but no actual strict typing 135|**Issue**: TypeScript strict mode is enabled but every function is typed `: any`. Strict mode adds no value because `: any` bypasses all checks. This gives a false sense of security. 136|**Fix**: Remove `strict: true` until proper types are added, OR add proper types. The current state is worse than no strict mode. 137| 138|### 29. Dockerfile has no HEALTHCHECK 139|**Issue**: Docker service has no way to know if the app is healthy. If the app starts but fails to serve (e.g., JSON file parse error), Docker thinks it's running fine. 140|**Fix**: Add `HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s CMD wget -qO- http://localhost:3000/ || exit 1` 141| 142|### 30. `package.json` has unused dependencies 143|**Issue**: `@ai-whisperers/admin`, `@ai-whisperers/auth`, `@ai-whisperers/commerce`, `@ai-whisperers/i18n`, `@ai-whisperers/seo`, `@ai-whisperers/ui`, `lucide-react` — these are imported but never used in any page or component (no icon imports, no admin UI, no auth, no i18n). They add ~50MB+ to the Docker image via node_modules. 144|**Fix**: Remove unused dependencies or make a cleanup pass. 145| 146|--- 147| 148|## ⚪ P3: LOW — nice to haves 149| 150|### 31. CTA buttons have no hover states 151|### 32. No keyboard navigation support (Tab, Enter, Escape on FAQ) 152|### 33. No focus-visible styles for accessibility 153|### 34. Page transitions would improve perceived performance 154|### 35. Structured data JSON-LD would help SEO 155|### 36. Loading states for server-rendered pages 156|### 37. Share buttons on blog posts 157|### 38. Print styles for /privacidad and /recursos 158|### 39. Docker compose should use healthcheck-based restart 159|### 40. CI pipeline should run TypeScript check + build test 160| 161|--- 162| 163|## SUMMARY 164| 165|| Priority | Count | Key items | 166||----------|-------|-----------| 167|| 🔴 P0 | 5 | Blog images broken, buildPageContent waste, readFileSync no cache, duplicate services, compliance section missing | 168|| 🟠 P1 | 9 | 0% TS types, pages router duplication, weak middleware, bad GallerySection typing, broken ServicesSection types, empty blog bodies, hardcoded footer logo, no error boundaries, missing pills component | 169|| 🟡 P2 | 16 | Terse variables, dead code, layout shift, tax calc hardcoded, Docker deps, style jsx overhead, fragile key resolution, duplicate loadJSON, no sitemap, no custom 404, no GA4 events, fake strict mode, no HEALTHCHECK, unused packages | 170|| ⚪ P3 | 10 | Accessibility, SEO, UX polish | 171|", "total_lines": 170, "file_size": 13719, "truncated": false, "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

{"content": "     1|# Nexa Paraguay — Complete Implementation Plan
     2|
     3|> Master todo list for taking nexa.paragu-ai.com from 2/10 to production-ready.
     4|> Every item is concrete, estimated, and actionable.
     5|
     6|---
     7|
     8|## TIER SYSTEM
     9|
    10|| Icon | Meaning |
    11||------|---------|
    12|| 🔴 **P0** | Blocks launch — conversion killer, legal risk, or 404 |
    13|| 🟠 **P1** | High impact — trust/corversion damage if missing |
    14|| 🟡 **P2** | Should do — quality polish, SEO, UX |
    15|| ⚪ **P3** | Nice to have — deferrable |
    16|
    17|---
    18|
    19|# SECTION 1: RENDER EXISTING CONTENT (no client input needed)
    20|
    21|These items unlock content that already exists in `es.json` but doesn't render because components are missing.
    22|
    23|## 1.1 New section components to build
    24|
    25|### FaqSection — /faq page
    26|- [ ] 🟠 **P1** Create `src/sections/FaqSection.tsx` — accordion component
    27|- [ ] 🟠 Render `faqPage.full.items` (15 Q&A pairs)
    28|- [ ] 🟠 Add open/close toggle, keyboard navigation, smooth height animation
    29|- [ ] 🟡 Add structured data `FAQPage` JSON-LD
    30|- [ ] 🟠 Add to SECTION_MAP in `[slug].tsx` and `index.tsx`
    31|
    32|### BlogSection — /blog page
    33|- [ ] 🟠 **P1** Create `src/sections/BlogSection.tsx` — blog post grid
    34|- [ ] 🟠 Render `blog.posts` (6 posts with title, date, excerpt, image)
    35|- [ ] 🟠 Each post card links to `/blog/[slug]`
    36|- [ ] 🟡 Add pagination or "Ver más" CTA
    37|- [ ] 🟠 Add to SECTION_MAP
    38|
    39|### BlogPostSection — individual blog post pages
    40|- [ ] 🟡 **P2** Create individual blog post rendering
    41|- [ ] 🟡 Each `blog.posts[i]` renders as standalone page at `/blog/[slug]`
    42|- [ ] 🟡 Author, date, category, tags, share buttons
    43|- [ ] 🟡 Related posts at bottom
    44|- [ ] 🟡 Add `BlogPosting` structured data
    45|
    46|### TeamSection — /sobre team section
    47|- [ ] 🟠 **P1** Create `src/sections/TeamSection.tsx` — team member cards
    48|- [ ] 🟠 Render `aboutPage.team.members` (5 members with name, role, description, image)
    49|- [ ] 🟠 Resolve `memberImage` through images manifest (currently broken 404)
    50|- [ ] 🟠 Grid layout with photo + name + role + description
    51|- [ ] 🟡 Add LinkedIn links when available
    52|- [ ] 🟠 Add to SECTION_MAP
    53|
    54|### PrivacyAccordion — /privacidad page
    55|- [ ] 🟠 **P1** Create `src/sections/PrivacyAccordion.tsx`
    56|- [ ] 🟠 Render `privacyPage.body.items` (5 GDPR/policy items)
    57|- [ ] 🟠 Accordion with open/close
    58|- [ ] 🟠 Add to SECTION_MAP and `privacidad` page config
    59|
    60|### GlossarySection — /glosario page
    61|- [ ] 🟡 **P2** Create `src/sections/GlossarySection.tsx`
    62|- [ ] 🟡 Render `glossaryPage.glossary.items` (7 terms)
    63|- [ ] 🟡 Card grid with term + definition
    64|- [ ] 🟡 Add A-Z filter or search
    65|- [ ] 🟡 Add to SECTION_MAP
    66|
    67|### GuidesSection — /recursos page
    68|- [ ] 🟡 **P2** Create `src/sections/GuidesSection.tsx`
    69|- [ ] 🟡 Render `resourcesPage.guides` with download CTAs
    70|- [ ] 🟡 Add actual PDF files (currently 404 on download links)
    71|- [ ] 🟡 Add to SECTION_MAP
    72|
    73|### PillarsSection — /por-que-paraguay, /calidad-de-vida, etc.
    74|- [ ] 🟠 **P1** Create `src/sections/PillarsSection.tsx` — generic pillar grid
    75|- [ ] 🟠 Render any `*.pillars` array with title, description, image, bullets
    76|- [ ] 🟠 Resolve `imageUrl` through images manifest
    77|- [ ] 🟠 Used by: whyCountryPage (9 pillars), qualityOfLifePage (4 pillars), aboutPage.whyNexa (7 pillars)
    78|- [ ] 🟠 Add to SECTION_MAP
    79|
    80|### ProcessTimeline — /proceso and landing pages
    81|- [ ] 🟡 **P2** Create `src/sections/ProcessTimeline.tsx` — visual timeline
    82|- [ ] 🟡 Render `*.process.steps` with images, duration, description
    83|- [ ] 🟡 Used by: /proceso, all landing pages (inversor, trust, lifestyle, empresa)
    84|- [ ] 🟡 Add to SECTION_MAP
    85|
    86|### WizardSection — /asistente (intake wizard)
    87|- [ ] ⚪ **P3** Build interactive intake questionnaire
    88|- [ ] ⚪ Multi-step form: country, income, objectives, timeline
    89|- [ ] ⚪ Program recommendation logic based on answers
    90|- [ ] ⚪ Lead capture at end
    91|
    92|### ComparisonSection — /comparacion page
    93|- [ ] ⚪ **P3** Build country comparison table
    94|- [ ] ⚪ Render `comparisonPage.matrix` (tax rates, cost of living, quality of life)
    95|- [ ] ⚪ PY vs UY vs PA vs EU comparison
    96|
    97|## 1.2 Fix image resolution in generic renderer
    98|- [ ] 🟠 **P1** `[slug].tsx` fallback already resolves `memberImage` and `imageUrl` through `resolveImage(images, ref)`
    99|- [ ] 🟠 Verify ALL `@src:` and `@img:` references in content are resolved, not used as raw URLs
   100|- [ ] 🟠 Check all slug pages for remaining raw `@src:` strings in HTML
   101|
   102|## 1.3 Update page configs to use new components
   103|- [ ] 🟠 **P1** Update `nexa-pages/faq.json` — use `faq` section component
   104|- [ ] 🟠 **P1** Update `nexa-pages/blog.json` — use `blog-index` section component
   105|- [ ] 🟠 **P1** Update `nexa-pages/privacidad.json` — use `privacy-accordion` section component
   106|- [ ] 🟠 **P1** Update `nexa-pages/sobre.json` — use `team` and `story` section components
   107|- [ ] 🟠 **P1** Update `nexa-pages/por-que-paraguay.json` — add pillar, trust, faq, cta sections
   108|- [ ] 🟡 **P2** Update `nexa-pages/calidad-de-vida.json` — add pillar, comparison, cta sections
   109|- [ ] 🟡 **P2** Update `nexa-pages/comparacion.json` — add comparison, cta sections
   110|- [ ] 🟡 **P2** Update `nexa-pages/glosario.json` — add glossary section
   111|- [ ] 🟡 **P2** Update `nexa-pages/recursos.json` — add guides section
   112|- [ ] 🟡 **P2** Update `nexa-pages/fundador.json` — add team section
   113|- [ ] 🟡 **P2** Update `nexa-pages/proceso.json` — ensure process timeline + highlights + faq + cta
   114|- [ ] 🟡 **P2** Update `nexa-pages/contacto.json` — add booking + contact details + map placeholder
   115|- [ ] 🟡 **P2** Update `nexa-pages/prensa.json` — add press list section
   116|
   117|---
   118|
   119|# SECTION 2: REPLACE PLACEHOLDER CONTENT (needs client input)
   120|
   121|## 2.1 Stats numbers — HOME / stats section
   122|- [ ] 🔴 **P0** `"+500 Familias reubicadas"` → Get real number or change to `"+100 familias confían en nosotros"`
   123|- [ ] 🔴 **P0** `"+10 Años de experiencia"` → Get real years or change to honest timeframe
   124|- [ ] 🔴 **P0** `"98% Tasa de éxito"` → Get real NPS/CSAT or remove entirely
   125|- [ ] 🟠 **P1** Stat icons — currently using Lucide icon names (Package, Plane, Users, Shield) that map to first-letter fallback. Wire up actual icon rendering or replace with brand-aligned SVGs
   126|
   127|## 2.2 Team — /sobre and /fundador
   128|- [ ] 🔴 **P0** Replace 5 role titles with real names
   129|- [ ] 🔴 **P0** Replace AI-generated placeholder headshots with real photos
   130|- [ ] 🔴 **P0** Add credentials: bar numbers (abogados), CPA registration (contadores)
   131|- [ ] 🟠 **P1** Add LinkedIn profile URLs for each team member
   132|- [ ] 🟠 **P1** Add brief bio (2-3 sentences each with relevant experience years)
   133|- [ ] 🟡 **P2** Group photo for team page
   134|
   135|## 2.3 Pricing — /programas and home
   136|- [ ] 🔴 **P0** Paraguay Base: `USD 2.900` → Confirm retail price or change to `Consultar`
   137|- [ ] 🔴 **P0** Paraguay Business: `USD 4.400+` → Confirm retail price
   138|- [ ] 🔴 **P0** Paraguay Investor: `USD 6.900+` → Confirm retail price
   139|- [ ] 🟠 **P1** Compra de Tierras: `Consultar` → Add price range or remove tier
   140|- [ ] 🟠 **P1** Price note semantics: "Honorarios, IVA y tasas incluidas" — legal review needed
   141|- [ ] 🟡 **P2** Add currency toggle USD/EUR for European prospects
   142|
   143|## 2.4 Testimonials — / and /casos-de-exito
   144|- [ ] 🔴 **P0** Replace 5 AI-generated portrait photos with nothing (quote-only) OR real photos with GDPR consent

{"content": "     1|# Nexa Paraguay — DNS cutover
     2|
     3|Target: `nexaparaguay.com` serves from Cloudflare Pages.
     4|
     5|## Preconditions
     6|
     7|- Domain `nexaparaguay.com` registered (Cloudflare Registrar recommended).
     8|- Cloudflare Pages project `nexa-paraguay` linked to `Main` branch.
     9|- Staging already green at `staging.nexaparaguay.com`.
    10|
    11|## Records to publish (production)
    12|
    13|| Type | Name | Target | Proxy |
    14||---|---|---|---|
    15|| CNAME | `@` (apex alias) | `<pages-project>.pages.dev` | Proxied (orange cloud) |
    16|| CNAME | `www` | `<pages-project>.pages.dev` | Proxied |
    17|| CNAME | `staging` | `<pages-project>-<preview-branch>.pages.dev` | Proxied |
    18|| TXT | `@` | SPF for transactional email — value depends on Resend/Postmark setup | — |
    19|| MX | `@` | Google Workspace MX set (if `[REDACTED_EMAIL]` is Google) | — |
    20|
    21|## Settings to verify
    22|
    23|- SSL/TLS → **Full (strict)**.
    24|- HTTPS → **Always use HTTPS** on.
    25|- Redirect rule: `http://www.nexaparaguay.com/*` → `https://nexaparaguay.com/$1` (301).
    26|- Page rule / Worker: none required — hostname rewrite handled in `web/middleware.ts`.
    27|
    28|## Cutover sequence
    29|
    30|1. Lower TTL on the current `nexaparaguay.com` A/CNAME record to **300s**, 24 hours before cutover (accelerates rollback).
    31|2. At cutover T-0: swap CNAME to Pages project, enable proxy, enable HTTPS, verify SSL certificate issued (auto via Cloudflare).
    32|3. T+5 min: `curl -I https://nexaparaguay.com` should return 200 from Cloudflare. If 522/525, wait or switch to DNS-only until cert issues.
    33|4. T+15 min: submit sitemaps per locale:
    34|   - `https://nexaparaguay.com/s/nl/nexa-paraguay/sitemap.xml`
    35|   - `https://nexaparaguay.com/s/en/nexa-paraguay/sitemap.xml`
    36|   - `https://nexaparaguay.com/s/de/nexa-paraguay/sitemap.xml`
    37|   - `https://nexaparaguay.com/s/es/nexa-paraguay/sitemap.xml`
    38|
    39|## Post-cutover
    40|
    41|- Enable Cloudflare Web Analytics (consent-gated by our banner).
    42|- Leave TTL at 300s for 72 hours, then raise to 3600s.
    43|- If problems: revert DNS to prior target — CDN caches are edge-only and flush fast.
    44|", "total_lines": 43, "file_size": 1998, "truncated": false, "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

messages.content:
{"content": "     1|# Nexa Paraguay — Social asset library
     2|
     3|Inventory of the 6 pre-rendered social templates in
     4|`sites/nexa-paraguay/images/social/`. Each is indexed in
     5|`sites/nexa-paraguay/images.json` under the `social.*` bucket.
     6|
     7|These are generic templates (no faces) — safe to ship without consent
     8|forms. See `docs/IMAGE_GENERATION_PROMPTS.md` § ethics for the general
     9|rule about synthetic people in marketing assets.
    10|
    11|## Assets
    12|
    13|| Manifest key          | Filename                 | Aspect | Recommended platform                                 | Caption angle                                                                 |
    14|| --------------------- | ------------------------ | ------ | [REDACTED_TOKEN] | [REDACTED_TOKEN]-------------- |
    15|| `social.villaMorra`   | `villa-morra.png`        | 1:1    | Instagram Feed (all markets), LinkedIn post          | Neighborhood deep-dive — "Where our clients actually live: Villa Morra."     |
    16|| `social.carmelitas`   | `carmelitas.png`         | 1:1    | Instagram Feed (all markets), Facebook post          | Neighborhood deep-dive — Carmelitas tree-lined boulevard lifestyle angle.    |
    17|| `social.sanBernardino`| `san-bernardino.png`     | 1:1    | Instagram Feed / Story (weekend content)             | Lifestyle — "El fin de semana fuera de Asunción: San Ber, el otro Paraguay." |
    18|| `social.dataTip`      | `data-tip.png`           | 1:1    | LinkedIn, Instagram carousel cover                   | Data / fact angle — territorial tax, 10% corporate, etc. Navy-champagne BG.  |
    19|| `social.btsOffice`    | `bts-office.png`         | 1:1    | Instagram, LinkedIn (team post)                      | Behind-the-scenes — office life, weekly huddle, authenticity angle.          |
    20|| `social.clientJourney`| `client-journey.png`     | 1:1    | LinkedIn, Instagram carousel cover                   | Process narrative — still-life showing the artifacts of a successful case.   |
    21|
    22|## Captions by locale — 4 seeds per template
    23|
    24|Seeds are kept under 220 chars so there's room to add context + hashtags
    25|inside Instagram's 2200-char limit. Rotate through the 4 options over
    26|successive weeks to avoid caption fatigue.
    27|
    28|### `social.villaMorra`
    29|
    30|**Español**
    31|1. "Villa Morra: donde nuestros clientes europeos realmente se instalan. Razones."
    32|2. "Una mañana en Villa Morra. El Paraguay que atrae inversión no es el de los titulares."
    33|3. "El barrio donde caminás a tu oficina, a la escuela de tus hijos y al supermercado. Villa Morra."
    34|4. "Villa Morra en números: cafés, torres corporativas, colegios internacionales — todo caminando."
    35|
    36|**English**
    37|1. "Where our European clients actually land: Villa Morra. Here's why."
    38|2. "A morning in Villa Morra. The Paraguay that attracts investment isn't the one in the headlines."
    39|3. "The neighbourhood where you walk to your office, your kid's school and the supermarket. Villa Morra."
    40|4. "Villa Morra in numbers: cafés, corporate towers, international schools — all walkable."
    41|
    42|**Nederlands**
    43|1. "Villa Morra: waar veel van onze klanten uiteindelijk landen. Waarom dat geen toeval is."
    44|2. "Een ochtend in Villa Morra. Het Paraguay dat investeringen aantrekt is niet het van de krantenkoppen."
    45|3. "De wijk waar je loopt naar kantoor, de school van je kinderen en de supermarkt. Villa Morra."
    46|4. "Villa Morra in cijfers: cafés, kantoortorens, internationale scholen — alles wandelbaar."
    47|
    48|**Deutsch**
    49|1. "Villa Morra: Wo unsere europäischen Kunden tatsächlich landen. Warum?"
    50|2. "Ein Morgen in Villa Morra. Das Paraguay, das Investitionen anzieht, ist nicht das der Schlagzeilen."
    51|3. "Das Viertel, wo Sie zu Büro, Schule und Supermarkt laufen. Villa Morra."
    52|4. "Villa Morra in Zahlen: Cafés, Bürotürme, internationale Schulen — alles fußläufig."
    53|
    54|---
    55|
    56|### `social.carmelitas`
    57|
    58|**Español**
    59|1. "Carmelitas en datos: familiar, caminable, internacional."
    60|2. "Carmelitas. Uno de los barrios más caminables de Asunción — árboles, embajadas, cafés sin turistas."
    61|3. "Por qué muchos de nuestros clientes terminan en Carmelitas: árboles, silencio, y el aeropuerto a 15 min."
    62|4. "Si Villa Morra es el norte financiero, Carmelitas es su norte residencial. Dos caras, una ciudad."
    63|
    64|**English**
    65|1. "Carmelitas in numbers: family-friendly, walkable, international."
    66|2. "Carmelitas. One of the most walkable neighbourhoods in Asunción — trees, embassies, tourist-free cafés."
    67|3. "Why many of our clients end up in Carmelitas: trees, silence, and the airport 15 minutes away."
    68|4. "If Villa Morra is the financial north, Carmelitas is the residential north. Two faces, one city."
    69|
    70|**Nederlands**
    71|1. "Carmelitas in cijfers: gezinsvriendelijk, compact, internationaal."
    72|2. "Carmelitas. Een van de meest wandelbare buurten van Asunción — bomen, ambassades, cafés zonder toeristen."
    73|3. "Waarom veel klanten eindigen in Carmelitas: bomen, stilte en luchthaven op 15 minuten."
    74|4. "Als Villa Morra het financiële noorden is, is Carmelitas het residentiële noorden. Twee gezichten, één stad."
    75|
    76|**Deutsch**
    77|1. "Carmelitas in Zahlen: familienfreundlich, fußläufig, international."
    78|2. "Carmelitas. Eines der fußgängerfreundlichsten Viertel Asuncións — Bäume, Botschaften, touristenfreie Cafés."
    79|3. "Warum viele Klienten in Carmelitas landen: Bäume, Stille und Flughafen 15 Minuten entfernt."
    80|4. "Wenn Villa Morra der finanzielle Norden ist, ist Carmelitas der residentielle Norden. Zwei Gesichter, eine Stadt."
    81|
    82|---
    83|
    84|### `social.sanBernardino`
    85|
    86|**Español**
    87|1. "El fin de semana respira en San Bernardino. La logística arranca el viernes."
    88|2. "San Bernardino. 45 minutos de Asunción, ritmo distinto, otra geografía mental."
    89|3. "Muchos clientes terminan eligiendo tener base acá y venir los fines de semana. Entendemos por qué."
    90|4. "San Ber en octubre: jacarandas, lago, terrazas. El Paraguay que no sale en los reportes del FMI."
    91|
    92|**English**
    93|1. "Weekends breathe in San Bernardino. The logistics plan starts Friday afternoon."
    94|2. "San Bernardino. 45 minutes from Asunción, different pace, different mental geography."
    95|3. "Many clients end up choosing a weekend base here. We get why."
    96|4. "San Ber in October: jacarandas, the lake, terraces. The Paraguay that doesn't make IMF reports."
    97|
    98|**Nederlands**
    99|1. "Het weekend ademt in San Bernardino. De logistiek begint hier op vrijdagmiddag."
   100|2. "San Bernardino. 45 minuten van Asunción, ander tempo, andere mentale geografie."
   101|3. "Veel klanten kiezen uiteindelijk voor een weekendbasis hier. Wij snappen waarom."
   102|4. "San Ber in oktober: jacaranda's, meer, terrassen. Het Paraguay dat niet in IMF-rapporten voorkomt."
   103|
   104|**Deutsch**
   105|1. "Das Wochenende atmet in San Bernardino. Die Logistik beginnt Freitagnachmittag."
   106|2. "San Bernardino. 45 Minuten von Asunción, anderes Tempo, andere geistige Geografie."
   107|3. "Viele Klienten entscheiden sich am Ende für eine Wochenend-Basis hier. Wir verstehen warum."
   108|4. "San Ber im Oktober: Jacarandas, See, Terrassen. Das Paraguay, das in IWF-Berichten fehlt."
   109|
   110|---
   111|
   112|### `social.dataTip`
   113|
   114|**Español**
   115|1. "Sistema territorial + 10% impuesto corporativo = previsibilidad."
   116|2. "Sistema fiscal territorial. En Paraguay, los ingresos generados fuera del país no pagan impuesto local."
   117|3. "Costo de vida vs. poder adquisitivo. El dato que cambia la ecuación para ingresos en euros o dólares."
   118|4. "Una sola residencia, acceso a MERCOSUR. Paraguay abre puertas operativas en Argentina y Brasil."
   119|
   120|**English**
   121|1. "Territorial tax + 10% corporate rate = predictability."
   122|2. "Territorial tax system. In Paraguay, foreign-sourced income is not locally taxed."
   123|3. "Cost of living vs. purchasing power. The data point that changes the maths for euro / dollar incomes."
   124|4. "One residency, MERCOSUR access. Paraguay opens operational doors in Argentina and Brazil."
   125|
   126|**Nederlands**
   127|1. "Territoriaal belastingsysteem + 10% vennootschapsbelasting = voorspelbaarheid."
   128|2. "Territoriaal belastingstelsel. In Paraguay wordt inkomen uit het buitenland lokaal niet belast."
   129|3. "Kosten van levensonderhoud vs. koopkracht. Het datapunt dat de rekensom verandert voor euro-inkomen."
   130|4. "Één residentie, MERCOSUR-toegang. Paraguay opent operationele deuren in Argentinië en Brazilië."
   131|
   132|**Deutsch**
   133|1. "Territorialsystem + 10% Körperschaftsteuer = Berechenbarkeit."
   134|2. "Territoriales Steuersystem. In Paraguay wird ausländisches Einkommen lokal nicht besteuert."
   135|3. "Lebenshaltungskosten vs. Kaufkraft. Der Datenpunkt, der die Rechnung für Euro-Einkommen verändert."
   136|4. "Eine Residenz, MERCOSUR-Zugang. Paraguay öffnet operative Türen in Argentinien und Brasilien."
   137|
   138|---
   139|
   140|### `social.btsOffice`
   141|
   142|**Español**
   143|1. "Lunes por la mañana en nuestra oficina de Asunción. Ningún cliente sin cara a cara."
   144|2. "Detrás de cámaras: la mayoría del trabajo no ocurre el día operativo, ocurre antes."
   145|3. "Prevalidación. Antes de que el cliente suba al avión, cada documento ya está revisado y apostillado."
   146|4. "Un equipo, una mesa. Abogados, contadores y coordinadores alineando un caso real."
   147|
   148|**English**
   149|1. "Monday morning in our Asunción office. No client without face-to-face."
   150|2. "Behind the scenes: most of the work doesn't happen on operational day — it happens before."
   151|3. "Pre-validation. Before our client boards the plane, every document is already reviewed and apostilled."
   152|4. "One team, one table. Lawyers, accountants and coordinators aligning on a real case."
   153|
   154|**Nederlands**
   155|1. "Maandagmorgen, ons team in Asunción. Geen klant zonder face-to-face."
   156|2. "Achter de schermen: het meeste werk gebeurt niet op de operationele dag — het gebeurt ervoor."
   157|3. "Prevalidatie. Voordat onze klant het vliegtuig instapt, is elk document al gecontroleerd en geapostilleerd."
   158|4. "Eén team, één tafel. Advocaten, accountants en coördinatoren stemmen een echte casus af."
   159|
   160|**Deutsch**
   161|1. "Montagmorgen in unserem Büro in Asunción. Kein Kunde ohne persönlichen Kontakt."
   162|2. "Hinter den Kulissen: Die meiste Arbeit passiert nicht am operativen Tag — sondern davor."
   163|3. "Vorvalidierung. Bevor unser Klient ins Flugzeug steigt, ist jedes Dokument geprüft und apostilliert."
   164|4. "Ein Team, ein Tisch. Anwälte, Steuerberater und Koordinatoren arbeiten an einem echten Fall."
   165|
   166|---
   167|
   168|### `social.clientJourney`
   169|
   170|**Español**
   171|1. "Pasaporte, cédula, carpeta de documentos, llave. Así se ve un caso cerrado."
   172|2. "De carpeta a cédula. Día operativo comprime en 8-10 horas lo que tradicionalmente requería 3 viajes."
   173|3. "El entregable real no son papeles — es tiempo. 8-12 semanas de principio a fin."
   174|4. "Foto después de firmar. El momento en que el cliente pasa de prospecto europeo a residente paraguayo."
   175|
   176|**English**
   177|1. "Passport, cédula, a folder of documents, a key. Here's what a closed case looks like."
   178|2. "From folder to ID card. Operational day compresses into 8-10 hours what traditionally required 3 trips."
   179|3. "The real deliverable isn't paperwork — it's time. 8-12 weeks end to end."
   180|4. "Post-signing snapshot. The moment our client goes from European prospect to Paraguayan resident."
   181|
   182|**Nederlands**
   183|1. "Paspoort, cédula, mapje met documenten, sleutel. Zo ziet een afgerond traject eruit."
   184|2. "Van map naar ID-kaart. De operationele dag comprimeert in 8-10 uur wat 3 reizen vergde."
   185|3. "De echte deliverable is geen papierwerk — het is tijd. 8-12 weken van begin tot eind."
   186|4. "Foto na de ondertekening. Het moment dat onze klant van Europese prospect verandert in Paraguayaans inwoner."
   187|
   188|**Deutsch**
   189|1. "Pass, Cédula, Dokumentenmappe, Schlüssel. So sieht ein abgeschlossener Fall aus."
   190|2. "Vom Ordner zum Ausweis. Der operative Tag verdichtet in 8-10 Stunden, was 3 Reisen erforderte."
   191|3. "Die echte Lieferung ist kein Papierkram — sondern Zeit. 8-12 Wochen von Anfang bis Ende."
   192|4. "Foto nach der Unterzeichnung. Der Moment, in dem unser Klient vom europäischen Interessenten zum paraguayischen Bewohner wird."
   193|
   194|## Technical notes
   195|
   196|- All assets are 1:1 PNG (best for Instagram Feed + LinkedIn post).
   197|- For Stories / Reels cut a 9:16 version from the same brief on demand.
   198|- Keep type safe within the middle 80% when cropping; platform UI chrome
   199|  eats the outer 10%.
   200|- Scheduler feed: run `npm run export:content-calendar -- nexa-paraguay`
   201|  to regenerate `sites/nexa-paraguay/dist/social/calendar.json` from
   202|  `docs/CONTENT_CALENDAR.yml`.
   203|- Related: `docs/ADS_ASSETS.md` for paid creatives and
   204|  `docs/IMAGE_GENERATION_PROMPTS.md` for the generation prompts.
   205|", "total_lines": 204, "file_size": 12454, "truncated": false, "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

{"content": "     1|{
     2|  "siteName": "Nexa Paraguay",
     3|  "tagline": "Your Paraguay move, guided by experience",
     4|  "placeholders": {
